summary_one_line: Input must contain a valid GPOS table (the literal 'GPOS' tag) so apply_gpos is set true

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A TrueType/OpenType font whose table directory carries the 4-byte ASCII tag 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) at a table-record offset, so the shaper plan sets this->apply_gpos and takes the true branch at line 263.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed places the exact bytes 47 50 4f 53 ('GPOS') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, the table tag the parser compares against to enable GPOS positioning; the W byte set at those offsets is a single concentrated value (G/P/O/S x20).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds never present the 'GPOS' tag at the table-record offsets — at 0x0c-0x0f the L byte sets are scattered low/high bytes (00, 01, 20, 2c, etc.) with no GPOS run, so apply_gpos stays false and the false-branch fallback path (apply_fallback_kern) runs instead.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CMP_MAP'd tag comparison from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the constant operand 'GPOS' into the input at matching offsets; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace so they must stumble onto the 4-byte tag by chance and never do across 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the GPOS-enable test in the shaper position routine, true side globally blocked.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique and the winner resolves 10/10 while the loser blocks 10/10.
The second decisive pair is also an I2S delta (vpc over vp).
Winner seeds carry the literal ASCII bytes GPOS at offset 0x0c onward.
At offsets 0x0c-0x0f the winner byte sets are a single concentrated GPOS run while loser sets are scattered.
Taking the true branch routes execution into map.position (GPOS apply); the false side runs the fallback-kern path.
The winner takes the true side (T=322) while the loser takes only the false side (F=381) at the blocker.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 263  T=322 F=0  T=0 F=381  if (this->apply_gpos)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets, unlike naive/value_profile.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it must find the tag by havoc/token mutation only.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5716 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 002c9ad92acce91b` and observed 2 I2S-floor rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 29 and 30 of the 31-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound dash signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose GPOS-enable gate compares against the 'GPOS' tag where naive/value_profile resolve the true branch as often as cmplog/vpc — showing the 4-byte tag substitution is not what gives I2S its edge.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows GPOS at offset 0x0c-0x0f but the prompt does not directly expose the parser's tag-comparison source, so the offset-to-table-record mapping is inferred from the seed structure rather than seen in code.
